overview: The Fruit Ripeness Project aims to develop an innovative sensor that detects changes in fruit skin impedance, accurately measuring fruit ripeness.
This product is designed for integration into fruit processing lines, enabling farmers to assess fruit ripeness swiftly.
This sensor will allow packing and distribution facilities to implement more effective food handling practices, reducing food waste and cutting operating costs.
longTermGoals: Design a commercially viable fruit ripeness sensor
Establish a complete fruit skin impedance and fruit ripeness database
Optimize ripeness sensor performance and cost
skills: Detection and processing of weak signals
quickPoints: We can track capacitance changes at the uF level
We intend to build a data processing engine based on ML

images.alt: Fruit ripeness sensor constructed from copper foil
images.caption: A fruit ripeness sensor constructed from copper foil, used to measure capacitance

images.alt: Benchtop impedance analyzer for fruit ripeness testing
images.caption: A benchtop impedance analyzer for measuring the capacitance of a load
